message:
🐛 Fix self-update mechanism resilience: timeouts, heartbeats, stale detection (#2056)

The self-update could get permanently stuck when kc-agent died mid-build,
leaving the UI on "Building kc-agent binary..." forever with no error.

Backend fixes (update_checker.go):
- Add hard timeouts on all build commands (git pull 2m, npm/builds 5m)
- Add heartbeat goroutine during builds so frontend knows agent is alive
- Capture build stdout/stderr so error messages show actual compiler output
- Build Go binaries to temp file then atomic rename (prevents corrupt binaries)
- Use absolute paths for binary output (fixes CWD-dependent path resolution)
- Set WaitDelay on exec.CommandContext to prevent hanging on pipe reads
- Inject exitFunc for testability of restart path

Frontend fixes (useUpdateProgress.ts):
- Add stale-state detection: if WebSocket disconnects during active update
  for >45s, auto-transition to failed state with helpful error message
- Track last message time and check periodically during active updates

Tests (update_checker_test.go):
- 10-iteration CI loop test for absolute reliability confidence
- Build timeout test (verifies killed after deadline)
- Build failure test (verifies compiler output in error broadcast)
- npm install retry test (verifies cache-clean retry logic)
- Git pull failure test (verifies immediate abort)
- Heartbeat test (verifies elapsed-time messages during long builds)
- Output capture test, tailLines test, buildErrorDetail test

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
